title: CAB default setup should use project schema (not plugin schema) for local CC integration
user_comment: we need to ensure our latest CAB don't have a default protocol to setup/validate CC CLI integration into projects by automatically using the distributable plugin schema as default (vs. initially setting it up as a local project with CC CLI integrated where the extensions are nested under project '.claude' path), as I'm noticing a legitimate inefficient UX friction. In hindsight, my personal conjecture of trying to wrap everything into a plugin from getgo will be more holistically practical, but the issue is that I can't load the project CC extensions unless I actually fully register the plugin to marketplace and enable; which is not I want necessarily as enabling external plugins are automatically loaded globally. So instead, our CAB default setup (whether for a brand new project codebase or existing project) should use the claude project schema where all extensions are neatly nested under the project '.claude' path within the project root to live alongside the codebase, ultimately cohesively serving as agentic OS platform (codebase + LLM CLI).
orchestrator_take: Plugin-first default is premature optimization for new-project integration. LL-23 proved plugin extensions require global marketplace registration to activate — wrong default for fresh/existing codebase integration. Correct default: project schema (.claude/ nested); plugin migration as later explicit opt-in phase.
evidence: Session 22 LL-23; user session-34 brain-dump; init-plugin vs new-project divergence

title: Command → skill migration audit (official CC deprecation of commands)
user_comment: per latest official CC dev docs, commands will be gradually be sunset and skills to be used/trigerred instead. luckily, our CAB hirearchical wrapping design philosophy, we already were pretty much effectively leveraging the commands to be essentially equivalent to the domain specialized skill extensions but just serving as the easy trigger mechanism by both CC agents and humans, via the dash function for any consistently repeated operations, so let's make sure we archive and no longer further optimize the commands and properly migrate to skills, if there isn't already the main pertaining skill(s) that was composing exist. we need to be strategic and careful here as I'm currently used to triggerring the skill-wrapped commands, not the skills themselves. let's start with a clear mapping of which existing skills are wrapped into commands and which aren't/disconnected, etc.
orchestrator_take: Strategic migration needed — CAB historically used commands as trigger-shims for skills. Step 1: produce mapping of existing skills ↔ commands (wrapped vs disconnected). Step 2: migrate orphan commands to skills. Keep command shims alive until user trigger habit transitions.
evidence: User ref to official CC dev docs; CAB commands/ inventory

title: Re-evaluate CAB orchestrator subagent as global-default CC agent
user_comment: we need to revisit our recent discussion (in session 34 i believe) of the Three-layer complement model architecture design philosophy: "global CLAUDEmd (global orchestrator as holistically efficient and generalized "master strategist" with universal or project-agnostic value-adds of memory, rules, skills, agents, hooks, which are technically and soundly domain-specialized of the intended global orchestrator via comprehensive, readily actionable context engineering protocols) → project CLAUDEmd (project orchestrator as holistically efficient and generalized "master strategist" with project-specific domain specialization/contextualization of CC extensions) → CAB's 'orchestrator' subagent being used as the "main default global cc agent" that I talk to per global cc settings JSON (please correct me if i'm misunderstanding here). Each layer is complementary, not redundant". I'm quite set on how I currently define and utilize the global core identity/SME vs. project core identify/SME via CLAUDEmds, but i'm still tad confused whether me setting CAB's specific 'orchestrator' subagent as the default CC global agent is the appropriate/efficient choice; i'm slightly leaning towards getting rid of this global cc setting to let the global CLAUDEmd and each project CLAUDEmd to properly serve as orchestrators.
orchestrator_take: Three-layer model is sound (global CLAUDEmd → project CLAUDEmd → domain subagents). Questioning whether CAB orchestrator subagent should be the GLOBAL default. Leaning toward removing global-default binding; let CLAUDEmd layers drive orchestration natively. Needs cost-benefit analysis of removal.

title: CAB advisor ↔ project-orchestrator agentic bridge architecture
user_comment: we need to further strategize how we can allow CAB orchestrator (or domain-specialized subagent as "general CAB advisor", which is fully equipped with the entire knowledge base via systematized context knowledge graph programmatically/visually) to agentically/seamlessly interact with each project domain orhcestrator(s) to ensure the CC integrated project has fully and systematically adopted CAB's various frameworks/design philosophies and context engineering protocols for holistically efficient orchestration and state management. basically, i've been finding myself in multiple occasions of active projects that have CC integrated where I would need to request the project orchestrator agent to refer back to some of the CAB KB packs as the existing agentic wrappers related to creating/integrating CC into new or existing projects, as well as validating/techdebt existing CC integration setup aren't fully capturing.
orchestrator_take: Capability gap: project orchestrators can't discover/invoke CAB KB packs agentically — forces manual re-prompting. Need bi-directional bridge: CAB advisor subagent (KB-equipped) that project orchestrators query, or MCP-exposed CAB KB endpoint. Cross-refs UXL-005 (knowledge graph) and UXL-009 (HydroCast patterns).

title: KB → knowledge-graph standardization (programmatic metadata for orchestration+state)

orchestrator_take: Root-cause framing for UXL-004/UXL-009/UXL-010: current KB is static markdown; graph-capable metadata (depends_on, related, source edges) exists in frontmatter but is not programmatically queryable. Needs: (a) graph extractor/indexer; (b) HydroCast-style programmatic notes↔knowledge linkage; (c) visualization surface. UXL-009 and UXL-010 seed this deep dive.

title: Agentic OS platform end-vision — skill=software=modularized-codebase deep dive
user_comment: User directive 2026-04-24 (embedded verbatim, end-vision note): "If we regard CAB as another 'traditional project' with a codebase, including src\, docs\, all the typical traditional codebase will consist of, where we then seamlessly integrate CC to be living alongside with the codebase to turn the project into an 'agentic OS platform' (codebase + LLM CLI), each domain-specialized skill will be the live, walking, iterative/dynamic KB packs where each eventually builds up to be skill = software = modularized codebase into specific domain-specialized skillsets, so the 'agentic OS platform' i keep coining will eventually effectively be merged into a single platform, where all core processors under the traditional codebase schema src\ will now be systematized into specialized CC skills (custom NL instruction for agentic reasoning in markdown and orchestration + utility scripts of the src\ + tailored KB cards into the skill's references folder programmatically). CAB is somewhat unique b/c we're jumping straight into this merged agentic OS platform vision since it didn't have an existing traditional codebase like GTA, Hydrocast projects for example. to clarify, this end-vision is not a strict requirement, and i'm not certain whether the native CC have the adequate capabilities for me to realize this practically today as well. I just wanted to have this in your purview as we push through the UX wave plan and re-thinking through many of our original/possibly outdated philosophies and system designs. i'd be curious whether you think the current skills list reflect this end-vision (aka do the existing skills and agents, along with the rules and memory, fully cover all aspects of static KB packs into agentic, readily actionables?)"
orchestrator_take: Strategic end-vision framing, not immediate actionable. User explicitly marks 'not a strict requirement' and 'not certain native CC has capabilities today.' Deferred to Wave 8+ per user framing: 'likely ways to go before we come across this phase task.' Intellectual ancestor referenced: ~/.claude/backups/_archive/rules-old/coding-philosophy/design-agent-pipeline.md (DP2's elaboration origin — skills housing both agentic NL instruction + deterministic utility scripts + references KB). Deep-dive scope when executed: (1) survey current 10 CAB skills for agentic-OS readiness — do they house scripts + references + orchestration logic per the skill=software equation?; (2) identify gaps where static-KB content hasn't been translated into agentic surface; (3) evaluate native CC capability against the end-vision (what works today vs what requires platform evolution); (4) produce end-vision roadmap with actionable near-term vs aspirational long-term split. Couples to UXL-005 (KG), UXL-037 (DP alignment), UXL-002 (commands→skills migration — which surfaces the skill-as-module pattern already).

title: Agent frontmatter valid-fields list drifted from current CC docs (memory, background, isolation, etc.)
user_comment: CC docs refresh (2026-04-22) during Phase 5 acceptance-criteria verification surfaced drift in .claude/rules/component-standards.md. CAB previously listed only {name, description, tools, model, effort, skills} as valid agent frontmatter fields, and marked disallowedTools as 'not a valid CC field'. Current docs (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents#supported-frontmatter-fields) list as valid: name, description, tools, disallowedTools, model, skills, effort, memory, background, isolation, color, initialPrompt, maxTurns, permissionMode, mcpServers, hooks. Plugin agents silently drop hooks/mcpServers/permissionMode. Rule updated in this same docs-refresh pass; this row captures the finding + source.
orchestrator_take: Rule updated in-session via .claude/rules/component-standards.md (see commit post Phase 5 closeout). New fields of strategic interest: `memory:` (enables subagent persistent memory — UXL-007/022/023), `isolation: worktree` (parallel-safe delegation), `background: true` (long-running agents). Templates/agents/ may need audit to adopt these new fields where beneficial — potential follow-on row.

title: Audit CAB agents to adopt memory: frontmatter field where cross-session learnings compound
user_comment: Audit existing CAB agents (agents/orchestrator.md, verifier.md, architecture-advisor.md, project-integrator.md + any others) for `memory:` field adoption. Per 2026-04-22 CC-docs refresh (UXL-031), subagent persistent memory is opt-in via `memory: user|project|local` frontmatter field. Agents that accumulate learnings across invocations (e.g., architecture-advisor gathering patterns, verifier collecting failure signatures) would benefit from `memory: project` for version-controlled team shareability. Per-agent scope decision needed: which agents have cross-session-valuable learnings vs stateless-per-invocation (most orchestrators). Deliverable: per-agent decision table + frontmatter updates + verification that MEMORY.md auto-injection works.
orchestrator_take: DEFERRED (coupled with UXL-022). Execute after UXL-022 authors the canonical CC Memory Layer Alignment KB card — spec-first prevents per-agent scope drift. Candidate adoption signals: (a) architecture-advisor → project (accumulates arch decisions across sessions); (b) verifier → project (failure signatures); (c) orchestrator → likely NONE (stateless-per-invocation by design; state lives in notes/); (d) project-integrator → user (cross-project integration patterns). Per-agent decision requires evidence of cross-session-valuable learnings — default to no-memory if unclear. → RESOLVED: Applied memory: to 3 CAB agents — architecture-advisor (project), verifier (project), project-integrator (user). Orchestrator SKIPPED per KB card anti-pattern rationale (stateless-per-invocation by design; state lives in notes/*.md).

Save: ~250-400 tokens per future resolution. → RESOLVED: Helper script shipped at hooks/scripts/uxl-update.py (resolve/set/enrich/sort/show commands). Active-top sort applied to CSV. title: Remove `effort: high` from 5 CAB skills — prevent Max-user silent effort downgrade
user_comment: UXL-002 Phase 1 findings (2026-04-24 CC-docs verification): 5 CAB skills (analyze-architecture, audit-workspace, execute-task, plan-implementation, scaffold-project) set `effort: high` in frontmatter. Per current CC docs (model-config page), Max subscription users on Opus 4.7 default to `xhigh` effort; skill frontmatter `effort:` overrides session level while skill is active. Net effect: Max users get SILENTLY DOWNGRADED from xhigh to high when any of these 5 skills fires — invisible UX quality regression. User directive 2026-04-24: remove `effort:` from these skills to let subscription-tier default drive (xhigh for Max, high for Pro). Override only when a specific reason to floor effort exists (none articulated for the 5 skills).
orchestrator_take: Scope: remove `effort: high` line from each of the 5 SKILL.md frontmatters. Net delta: no user-facing trigger change; user's configured effort drives instead of hard-coded `high`. Max users recover xhigh default; Pro users still get high (unchanged). Also updates component-standards.md to document the new CAB convention (UXL-040 couples). Phase 3a deliverable per user directive 2026-04-24. → RESOLVED: Removed effort:high from 5 skills per user directive 2026-04-24 Q1. Max users on Opus 4.7 now receive their xhigh default when these skills fire (recovered from silent downgrade to high). Pro users unaffected. Commit fdfea25. Couples UXL-040 (rule refresh).
